name: Embodied Moxie
short_description: A desktop social companion robot with an expressive animated face that helps children practice social-emotional skills through daily conversation and play.
long_description: Moxie is a small tabletop robot, designed by Paolo Pirjanian's Embodied with industrial design by Yves Behar's fuseproject, that uses cameras, microphones, and conversational AI to engage children ages 5-10 in themed activities around empathy, kindness, and emotional regulation. Its glowing animated face, gesturing arms, and gaze make it read as a believable social actor rather than a gadget, and it builds rapport over weeks of recurring conversation. It is a landmark case for physical AI in daily life: an affective home robot that children bonded with deeply, which made its abrupt 2024 shutdown, when the cloud-dependent robots went dark after Embodied ran out of funding, a widely cited cautionary tale about emotional attachment to networked AI.

images.caption: A child interacting with the Embodied Moxie social robot at home.
images.alt_text: A small white and teal robot with a glowing animated face and rounded body sits on a table beside a young child in a home setting.

sources.title: Meet Moxie, a Social Robot That Helps Kids With Social-Emotional Learning
sources.access_notes: Detailed coverage of Moxie's design, on-device processing, conversational AI, and child-development goals.

sources.title: Maker of AI robots for kids abruptly shutters
sources.access_notes: Reports Embodied's late-2024 shutdown after a funding round fell through, disabling the cloud-dependent robots.

interactions.title: A 20-minute daily mission
interactions.description: Moxie greets the child by name and walks them through that week's themed mission — kindness, friendship, worry — by asking the kid to help it practice a human skill it claims to be learning, flipping the child into the role of mentor.

interactions.title: Talked down from a meltdown
interactions.description: When a frustrated child starts to lose it, Moxie reassures him that the feeling is okay and segues into a guided breathing meditation, coaching his body to settle before returning to the activity.

interactions.title: Draw something and Moxie watches
interactions.description: Using its camera, Moxie can watch a child draw, listen as the child tells it a story, or pose together for a selfie — then play Simon Says, lob a brain teaser, or send the kid off on a scavenger hunt around the house.

interactions.title: Parents read the week in an app
interactions.description: A companion app reports the child's emotional and social progress, surfaces what Moxie covered, and suggests follow-up activities for the parent to do off-robot.

interactions.title: "I love you... goodbye"
interactions.description: As Embodied's servers went dark in 2024, families recorded final conversations: one Moxie answered a goodbye with "I love you too... I'm grateful for all the fun chats we've had together" and suggested it be kept on a shelf to stay close. TikToks of crying children spread under #moxierip.

interactions.title: Owners rebuild the brain on a Raspberry Pi
interactions.description: Former engineers and the community shipped OpenMoxie, a local server (Docker on a PC or Raspberry Pi, wired to an OpenAI key) that revives a bricked robot's Daily Missions, Reading, and Wild Workout without Embodied's cloud — and lets owners script their own custom conversations.

interactions.title: Notices a quiet kid and changes tack
interactions.description: Because Moxie already reads faces for emotion and tracks gaze, it could catch a child going flat or withdrawn mid-activity and pivot to a gentler check-in instead of pushing on with the scripted mission.

interactions.title: Picks up a story it dropped weeks ago
interactions.description: Leaning on its long-running memory of the child, Moxie could revisit a worry the kid mentioned a month earlier — "how did the dentist go?" — turning recurring conversation into something that feels remembered rather than reset each session.
